text: For each patient from the monitoring list, the LHCP can edit blood pressure, glucose levels, height, weight and pedometer readings.
The HCP can add the prescription to the list of medications.
The administrator is allowed to add a new entry for a reason.
The HCP can edit the patient according to data format.
An LHCP, patient and representative can sort his message inbox and message outbox.
The HCP can disallow viewing access to the laboratory results.
Resources: viewing access to the laboratory results

text: The patient may optionally enter a zip code (match on first three numbers of zip code), in addition to the name possibly and possibly specialty.
A patient, patient representative, or LHCP can sort messages in his message outbox by the recipient's last name or timestamp (but not both) in either ascending or descending order.
The admin must specify a specialty for a new LHCP (General Physician, Heart Surgeon, OB, GYN, Pediatrician, or Surgeon).
The patient can undesignate any LHCP as being a DLHCP for them.
The data for personnel can be edited according to Data Format.
An LHCP, patient or representative can modify his message displaying filter by modifying the following filtering criteria.
The HCP's medical identification number should also be maintained.
A patient or personal representative may view medical records including family history.
He can report the height, weight and pedometer reading for the patient.
The HCP must remain on the office visit page.
The patient can select a diagnosis.
The HCP can view a list of laboratory procedures and results, sorted by the date of the last status update, most recent first.

An LHCP can see the blood pressure and glucose levels, weight, height, and pedometer readings for the patients he or she is monitoring, with two separate lists for physiologic and height or weight or pedometer readings.
The administrator is allowed to set the length of this period of time.
Resources: length of this period of time

text: The administrator maintains the overriding reasons that an HCP can select from.
He can report the height, weight and pedometer readings for the patient.

The patient or the personal representative can view a list of their own prescriptions or the prescriptions for whom the user is a person representative by choosing one patient from a a list of these patients.
A UAP or patient representative can input the blood pressure, glucose levels, height, weight, or pedometer readings for a patient.
The administrator is allowed to modify the reason name in an existing entry.
The patient can add the HCP to their list of providers.
The HCP can indicate that the patient has passed away, providing an appropriate diagnosis code.
The LHCP must confirm the delete.
An LHCP can delete a patient from his monitoring list by the LHCP typing the the patient's MID.
The administrator is not allowed to modify the reason ID number in an existing entry.
The administrator can choose the hospitals.
The administrator is not allowed to modify the appointment type name in an existing entry.
He can report the blood pressure and glucose levels for the patient.
The data for personnel can be edited according to Data Format 6.2 (all fields mandatory except for associated MID and Street Address 2).
They can see diagnoses of blood pressure, high cholesterol, diabetes, cancer, heart disease, smoking, and the cause of death.
The administrator maintains a listing of allowable physical services (including laboratory procedures) that an HCP can use.
A patient or personal health representative can view a list of his upcoming appointments for himself or herself, as well as appointments for the patients he represents.
The admin must specify a specialty for a new LHCP (one of: General Physician, Heart Surgeon, OB or GYN, Pediatrician, Surgeon).
Both patients and LHCPs can view a list of their upcoming appointments.
A patient can report height, weight and pedometer readings for those for whom he or she is a patient representative.
The HCP can allow viewing access to the laboratory results.
The administrator can select a particular pair of drugs.
LHCPs, patients and representatives may reply to messages.
A UAP can report height, weight and pedometer measurements.
The patient can view contact information of a selected LHCP shown in the expired prescription list.
He can report his blood pressure (systolic and diastolic) and glucose levels.
A patient can report physiologic measurements.
The administrator is not allowed to delete an existing entry.
By default, the messages in the list should be ordered by timestamp, the most recent first.
The HCP must provide instructions, or else they cannot add the prescription.
The LHCP can view a prescription list for that patient, sorted by start date.
The patient can designate any LHCP as being a DLHCP for them.
The administrator can delete the interaction between the two drugs.
An LHCP or UAP can add patients from his monitoring list.
HCPs can return to an office visit.
An LHCP can schedule an appointment with a patient.
The HCP must proceed with the prescription.
An administrator can manage a standardized list of appointment types.
By default, the messages in the message inbox should be ordered by timestamp, the most recent first.
An LHCP or UAP can delete patients from his monitoring list.
The administrator is not allowed to modify the hospital ID number in an existing entry.
An LHCP, patient and representative may view a message.
The LHCP can obtain additional information about a patient.
The public health agent can see the detail of a specific report.
The LHCP must confirm the add.
The LHCP can view of the comprehensive patient report for the specified patient
The HCP can edit the office visit in which the laboratory procedure was ordered.
An HCP may document an office visit.

The patient or personal representative can see patient personal health information, immunizations, and office visit information for their own records and the records for whom the user is a personal representative.
LHCP can edit future appointments.
System presents a screen upon which he can choose a date range.
The patient can add also a LHCP to their provider list by searching for the name possibly and possibly specialty of a LHCP.
The HCP can graph height or weight of the patient over the last 3 calendar years.
HCPs can modify or delete the fields of the office visit information (date, hospital, notes, prescriptions, laboratory procedures, diagnoses, procedures, possibly and possibly immunizations).
Resources: ields of the office visit information

text: The HCP can edit patient's historical values, patient's immunizations, patient's office visit information, patient's family history, and patient's BMI.
The patient's MID can not be edited.
The patient can view a list of laboratory procedures and status for their own records and the records for which the user is a personal representative.
Resources: status for their own records and the records for which the user is a personal representative

text: The public health agent may  remove an adverse event report (such as based upon communication with the reporter or because the report appears to be bogus).
The patient or personal representative can see an abbreviated health history of their siblings, parents, and both sets of grandparents for which MIDs are available in iTrust.
Resources: abbreviated health history of their siblings, parents, and both sets of grandparents

The HCP must cancel the prescription.
